sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presented with a history of left-sided back pain that varies in intensity, described as sharp on some days and non-existent on others.
He reports no fever, unchanged appetite, and an increase in urine output.
He denies being more tired than usual.
He has a history of recurrent strep throat, with four episodes in the last five months, for which he has been treated by his primary care physician.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of recurrent strep throat, with four episodes in the last five months.
He has a brother who smokes.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's throat is not erythematous, and his tonsils are midline.
There is no lymphadenopathy or cervical adenopathy.
Lung sounds are clear, with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
Heart sounds are normal, with no murmur, rub, or gallop.
The patient does not experience pain when pressing on the left side of the back.
The abdomen is not tender to palpation.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent strep throat, which may be caused by glomerular nephritis, a condition that could lead to chronic kidney disease.
Problem list: Glomerulonephritis; Suspected; Recurrent strep throat; Confirmed; Past strep throat episodes; Confirmed.
Plan:
I referred the patient to an ENT specialist to consider tonsillectomy to help manage his recurrent strep throat.
I ordered several laboratory tests, including a lipid panel, UA, CMP, CBC, vitamin D, and TSH, to evaluate his hormonal and nutritional status.
The patient will have these tests done as soon as possible, with the exception of those requiring fasting, which will be done today.
I will write a note to excuse him from his class and work to accommodate the testing schedule.
We will discuss the possibility of a new job affecting his access to free food, which may impact his salt intake.
I will also have a conversation with his brother about smoking.
Follow-up: The patient will return in a week for the results of the laboratory tests.
